rulings.Case Title: CLEM FITZHUGH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR MONDAY, MARCH 3, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for plaintiff¿s alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant¿s Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to plaintiff. Plaintiff¿s objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. The parties have stipulated that the oral arguments made on February 26, 2014 in the hearings for Godbee, Schlimmer, Santa, Rieser and Bettencourt apply to this motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. on Wednesday, March 5, 2014, not March 3, 2014. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
